content.explanation:
**May Have — Past Possibility (50% Certainty):**

"May have" = शायद...हुआ हो (50% possibility — you think it happened but not sure)

**Structure:** Subject + may have + Past Participle (V3)

🇮🇳 वह शायद घर चला गया हो।
🇬🇧 He **may have gone** home.

🇮🇳 उसने शायद phone switch off किया हो।
🇬🇧 She **may have switched** off her phone.

**Negative:** Subject + **may not have** + V3
🇮🇳 शायद उसे पता नहीं था।
🇬🇧 He **may not have known** about it.

**May Have vs Might Have:**
- May have = 50% possibility (more likely)
- Might have = 30% possibility (less likely, more remote)
rules: May have + V3 = 50% past possibility (moderate uncertainty)
May not have + V3 = negative past possibility
Used when you have some reason to believe it happened, but not 100% sure
Compare: Must have (99%), May have (50%), Might have (30%)
Different from "may have to" — that means "possibly need to" (present/future)
Question: "Could he have...?" not "May he have...?" (questions use could/might)
content.memoryTrick: **May Have = Coin Flip 🪙** — May have का मतलब है 50-50 chance। जैसे coin flip करो — head आए या tail, कह नहीं सकते। 'He may have called' = 50% chance उसने call किया।

examples.hindi: वे शायद पहले ही निकल गए हों।
उसे शायद address पता नहीं था।
examples.english: He may not have known the address.

examples.hindi: वह शायद busy रही हो इसीलिए reply नहीं किया।
examples.english: She may have been busy, that is why she did not reply.

examples.hindi: Package शायद deliver हो गया हो।
examples.english: The package may have been delivered.

examples.hindi: उसने शायद आपका message पढ़ा ही नहीं हो।
examples.english: He may not have read your message.

examples.hindi: Meeting शायद cancel हो गई हो।
examples.english: The meeting may have been cancelled.

mistakes.correct: Could he have arrived? / Has he arrived?
mistakes.why: "May have" questions use could/might, not may
Negation: "may not have" — not "may have not"

speakingTips: Giving benefit of doubt: 'She may have had an emergency — let's wait.'
When you're not sure: 'The files may have been accidentally deleted.'
Professional setting: 'The client may have missed the email — let's follow up.'

vocabulary.example: I am uncertain — she may have known.
It is possible — he may have arrived.
Apparently, he may have changed his plan.
Seemingly, things may have gone wrong.
He supposedly may have been informed.
Presumably, she may have taken a different route.
We can only speculate — he may have left early.

practice.hindi: वह शायद घर चला गया हो।
practice.english: He may have gone home.
alternatives: he might have left for home.

practice.hindi: वह शायद busy रही हो।
practice.english: She may have been busy.
alternatives: she might have been occupied.

practice.hindi: Package शायद deliver हो गया हो।
practice.english: The package may have been delivered.
alternatives: the parcel might have arrived.

practice.hindi: Meeting शायद cancel हो गई हो।
practice.english: The meeting may have been cancelled.
alternatives: the meeting might have been called off.

practice.hindi: उसने शायद आपका message नहीं पढ़ा हो।
practice.english: He may not have read your message.
alternatives: he might not have seen your message.

practice.hindi: वे शायद पहले ही निकल गए हों।
practice.english: They may have already left.
alternatives: they might have already departed.

practice.hindi: शायद उसे address पता नहीं था।
practice.english: He may not have known the address.
alternatives: he might not have had the address.

practice.hindi: वह शायद train से गई हो।
practice.english: She may have gone by train.
alternatives: she might have taken the train.

practice.hindi: शायद उन्होंने हमारी plan के बारे में सुना हो।
practice.english: They may have heard about our plan.
alternatives: they might have found out about our plan.

practice.hindi: शायद वह interview में select हो गई हो।
practice.english: She may have been selected in the interview.
alternatives: she might have cleared the interview.

practice.hindi: Client शायद पहले ही email देख चुका हो।
practice.english: The client may have already seen the email.
alternatives: the client might have already read the email.

practice.hindi: शायद उसे अभी तक नहीं पता।
practice.english: He may not have found out yet.
alternatives: he might not have been informed yet.

practice.hindi: वे शायद already decide कर चुके हों।
practice.english: They may have already decided.
alternatives: they might have made up their minds.

practice.hindi: शायद उसका phone switch off था।
practice.english: His phone may have been switched off.
alternatives: his phone might have been turned off.

practice.hindi: वह शायद अभी तक office में ही हो।
practice.english: She may have still been in the office.
alternatives: she might still be at the office.
practice.hint: may have + been in the office

practice.hindi: शायद वे किसी और route से गए हों।
practice.english: They may have gone by a different route.
alternatives: they might have taken another road.
practice.hint: may have + gone by different route

practice.hindi: शायद उसे promotion मिल गई हो।
practice.english: He may have gotten a promotion.
alternatives: he might have been promoted.

practice.hindi: Flight शायद delay हो गई हो।
practice.english: The flight may have been delayed.
alternatives: the flight might have been postponed.

practice.hindi: शायद वह already plan change कर चुका हो।
practice.english: He may have already changed his plan.
alternatives: he might have revised his plans already.

practice.hindi: वे शायद नए office में shift हो गए हों।
practice.english: They may have shifted to a new office.
alternatives: they might have moved to a new office.

practice.hindi: शायद उसे emergency थी।
practice.english: She may have had an emergency.
alternatives: she might have faced an urgent situation.

practice.hindi: वह शायद exam में pass हो गया हो।
practice.english: He may have passed the exam.
alternatives: he might have cleared the exam.

practice.hindi: शायद उन्होंने already decision ले लिया हो।
practice.english: They may have already made the decision.
alternatives: they might have already settled the matter.

practice.hindi: वह शायद नया laptop खरीद लिया हो।
practice.english: She may have bought a new laptop.
alternatives: she might have purchased a new laptop.

practice.hindi: शायद file गलती से delete हो गई हो।
practice.english: The file may have been accidentally deleted.
alternatives: the file might have been deleted by mistake.

practice.hindi: वह शायद बीमार हो गया हो।
practice.english: He may have fallen ill.
alternatives: he might have gotten sick.

practice.hindi: शायद उन्हें अभी तक confirmation नहीं मिली हो।
practice.english: They may not have received the confirmation yet.
alternatives: they might not have gotten the confirmation yet.

practice.hindi: वह शायद event में नहीं आई हो।
practice.english: She may not have come to the event.
alternatives: she might not have attended the event.

practice.hindi: शायद report already submit हो गई हो।
practice.english: The report may have already been submitted.
alternatives: the report might have been sent already.

practice.hindi: वह शायद already city छोड़ चुका हो।
practice.english: He may have already left the city.
alternatives: he might have already moved out of the city.
practice.hint: may have + already + left the city
